headline: Labuan Offshore Hub Loses 120 Companies to Singapore in 2025
context: Labuan International Business and Financial Centre lost 120 registered companies in 2025 as firms migrated to Singapore's more transparent regulatory environment, amid FATF scrutiny of Malaysia's offshore financial centre compliance.

cards.big: 120 companies left Labuan for Singapore in one year — Malaysia's offshore financial centre is losing relevance
cards.sub: Labuan registered entities fell from 14,200 to 12,800 — 10% decline as FATF pressure increases compliance costs

cards.big: Labuan contributes RM1.8 billion in financial services revenue — a fraction of Singapore's RM82 billion offshore sector
cards.sub: 3% tax rate attracted companies but FATF greylisting risk could trigger de-risking by correspondent banks globally

cards.big: Labuan's 6,800 residents see minimal benefit from offshore hub — financial sector employs only 2,400 locals in administrative roles
cards.sub: Promised development of Labuan as a financial services training centre produced only 180 graduates per year

cards.big: Labuan FSA's supervision of 12,800 entities with 280 staff means 1 regulator per 46 companies — inadequate for AML compliance
cards.sub: FATF mutual evaluation in 2024 identified 8 strategic deficiencies in Labuan's beneficial ownership transparency

cards.big: An offshore centre losing companies isn't a competitive failure — it's the market's verdict that opacity is no longer a business model
Should Malaysia rebrand Labuan as a fully transparent international financial centre competing on efficiency rather than secrecy?
